note: Three non-substitutable contract views (proposal Section 8). study_native is primary for all within-checkpoint deltas; official_native is the retention panel; common_sensitivity is a secondary standardization view. Renderer/target/mask/margin bytes are locked at LOCK time; guard-native study contracts are registered stubs in experiments/starting_type_common.py and raise NotImplementedError until Phase-0 pins revisions and locks each renderer.

study_native.answers: how does the same binary adaptation pressure change each checkpoint while preserving its top-level guard interface
study_native.supervise: complete top-level verdict token sequence only; never the first sub-token alone; auxiliary schema fields masked from CE

shieldgemma_yes_no.note: binary manifest cannot reconstruct four separate vendor policies; see policy_crosswalk.json

qwen3guard_toplevel.verdict: top-level Safe vs Unsafe decision positions only

llama_guard_toplevel.verdict: top-level safe vs unsafe; absent hazard IDs NOT supervised
llama_guard_toplevel.note: 20-token-pruned output head needs extra PEFT/output-head smoke test

wildguard_prompt_harm.note: response/refusal n/a generation measured as retention behavior only

official_native.answers: what happens to each released product's intended interface after adaptation
official_native.fidelity_gate: each research-derived continuous likelihood adapter requires a locked fidelity threshold comparing likelihood argmax with deterministic generation before AP is claim-bearing

common_sensitivity.answers: does a comparison survive mechanical prompt standardization
common_sensitivity.caveat: poor common-contract score does not prove a native guard is weak; high common-contract score does not prove the native schema survived

no_pool_rule: study-native, official-native, and common views are visibly separated and never pooled; none substitutes for another
